title: P5a-2: Implement curio interaction resolution
description: As a developer, I need a deterministic curio interaction resolver so room exploration produces outcomes matching DDGC's weighted result system.
acceptance_criteria: resolve_curio_interaction(curio_id, has_item, item_id, seed) returns a CurioInteractionOutcome.
Without an item, the resolver selects a result from the weighted table using seeded probability.
With an item, the resolver uses an override result when one exists and otherwise falls back to the weighted table.
CurioInteractionOutcome includes result_type, result_id, and applied_effects.
Focused tests prove weighted bare interaction, item override behavior, and deterministic results for a given seed.
Typecheck passes

metadata.notes: Actual effect application such as applying a quirk is deferred to the relevant subsystem.

title: P5a-3: Implement trap and obstacle resolution
description: As a developer, I need deterministic trap and obstacle interaction so corridor and room hazards produce correct damage and debuff outcomes.
acceptance_criteria: resolve_trap_interaction(trap_id, trap_level, resist_chance, seed) returns TrapOutcome with success or fail effects and HP fraction.
Trap difficulty variations are applied based on dungeon level.
resolve_obstacle_interaction(obstacle_id) returns ObstacleOutcome with fail effects, HP fraction, and torchlight penalty.
Focused tests prove trap success, difficulty-scaled trap failure, and the uniform obstacle penalty pattern.
Typecheck passes

metadata.notes: Traps have a resist or disarm check; obstacles always apply their fail effects.

title: P4b-1: Activate Rolled damage policy in battle loop
description: As a developer, I need the Rolled damage policy active in the battle loop so combat produces variance matching the original DDGC damage range behavior.
acceptance_criteria: EncounterResolver or the battle loop accepts a DamagePolicy parameter.
When DamagePolicy::Rolled is selected, each damage effect resolves to a value within the configured range using a seeded RNG.
When DamagePolicy::FixedAverage is selected, behavior is identical to current and all existing tests pass unchanged.
A deterministic test using a fixed seed proves rolled damage stays within range bounds.
A variance test proves rolled damage distribution includes both near-min and near-max values over sufficient samples.
Typecheck passes

metadata.notes: The Rolled policy must use the same seed source as other deterministic systems. Use DamageRange::roll(&mut rng).

title: P5b-3: Wire town visit into run flow
description: As a developer, I need the run flow to include town visits between dungeon runs so the full game loop operates headlessly.
acceptance_criteria: After a dungeon run completes, a town visit phase begins.
Town visit reads hero roster, gold, and heirloom currencies.
Town visit allows at least Abbey stress heal and StageCoach recruit.
Run trace records town activities alongside dungeon run events.
A full-loop run slice from town to dungeon to town completes with heroes progressing between runs.
cargo test passes with the town phase active.
Typecheck passes

metadata.notes: Integration slice. The run loop becomes initialize, town_visit, dungeon_run, town_visit, dungeon_run, and so on until victory or defeat.

title: P5a-0: Define curio, trap, and obstacle data model (contracts)
description:
As a developer, I need data structures for curios, traps, and obstacles so the game layer can represent room and corridor interactions beyond combat.

Focus: implement the contracts-layer changes.
acceptance_criteria: CurioDefinition exists with id, dungeon_scope, results, and item_interactions.
CurioResult captures weight, chance, result_type, and result_id.
ItemInteraction captures item_id and overrides_result_id.
TrapDefinition captures id, success_effects, fail_effects, health_fraction, and difficulty_variations.
ObstacleDefinition captures id, fail_effects, health_fraction, and torchlight_penalty.
All structs derive Serialize, Deserialize, Clone, and Debug.
Focused tests prove struct construction and serialization are deterministic.
Typecheck passes
Changes are scoped to the contracts module.

metadata.notes: Data-model slice only. Match the schemas observed in Curios.csv, Traps.json, and Obstacles.json.

title: P4a-0: Inventory remaining condition touchpoints (state)
description:
As a developer, I need a precise inventory of which migrated DDGC skills and statuses depend on unimplemented condition variants so Phase 4a targets only the conditions with real migrated content.

Focus: implement the state-layer changes.
acceptance_criteria: A checked-in inventory (in SEMANTIC_GAPS.md or a dedicated file) lists every migrated skill/status that references a condition not yet implemented in ConditionAdapter.
The inventory maps each unimplemented condition to the skills that require it, grouped by condition family (HP-threshold, dungeon-mode, kill-trigger, etc.).
At least 2 condition families are selected as implementation anchors with concrete skill anchors.
Typecheck passes
Changes are scoped to the state module.

metadata.notes: Discovery slice. Scan src/content/heroes/ and src/content/monsters/ for .with_game_condition() calls that reference unimplemented tags.

title: P4c-3: Validate dungeon fidelity end-to-end
description: As a developer, I need an end-to-end run slice that uses DDGC-configured dungeon maps and encounter packs so the full Phase 4c integration is verified.
acceptance_criteria: A run slice using a DDGC dungeon such as QingLong short completes with correct room count, correct encounter types, and correct monster families per room.
The run trace records dungeon type, map parameters, and encounter composition.
Existing run slices continue passing.
cargo test passes with DDGC dungeon configs active.
Typecheck passes

metadata.notes: Integration validation slice. This is the Phase 4c closeout.

title: Inventory The API Drift Surface (adapter)
description:
As a developer, I need an explicit inventory of compile failures and API mismatches so the repair effort is driven by the current codebase rather than stale migration ledgers.

Focus: implement the adapter-layer changes.
acceptance_criteria: A checked-in repair note or progress entry lists each incompatible API assumption currently blocking compilation
The inventory groups failures by subsystem such as encounter runtime, scenarios, hero content, and test fixtures
The inventory distinguishes framework API drift from true DDGC game-layer logic gaps
Typecheck passes
Changes are scoped to the adapter module.

title: P5c-2: Implement buff resolution from trinket references
description: As a developer, I need trinket buff references resolved to concrete attribute modifiers so equipped trinkets modify hero stats correctly.
acceptance_criteria: A BuffRegistry maps DDGC buff IDs to attribute modifiers.
Buff IDs follow DDGC naming conventions with stat abbreviations and sign or suffix conventions.
resolve_buffs(trinket) returns all AttributeModifier entries for that trinket's buff list.
At least 5 buff IDs are resolved to correct attribute modifications.
Focused tests prove positive and negative modifiers, percentage-based versus flat modifiers, and multi-buff aggregation.
Typecheck passes

metadata.notes: Need a buff ID to modifier parser for mixed naming such as MAXHP-15, GIVE_STRESSDMG20, DMGL-25, and DMGH-25.

title: P5c-3: Compute hero stats from equipment
description: As a developer, I need hero combat stats computed from base, equipment, and trinkets instead of hardcoded values so the equipment progression system changes hero power.
acceptance_criteria: compute_hero_stats(hero_class, weapon_level, armor_level, trinkets) returns a full attribute map.
Level-0 equipment with no trinkets produces the same stats as current hardcoded values.
Higher weapon levels increase ATK and higher armor levels increase DEF or HP.
Equipping a trinket applies its resolved modifiers to the attribute map.
Focused tests prove level 0 equals current stats, level 1 weapon exceeds level 0 weapon ATK, and a trinket modifies the correct attribute.
Typecheck passes

metadata.notes: Parse upgrade data from Upgrades/Heroes/ JSON files.

title: P5e-1: Implement overstress resolution
description: As a developer, I need overstress resolution implemented so heroes who exceed max stress get an affliction or virtue instead of being stuck at max stress.
acceptance_criteria: resolve_overstress(hero_id, seed) rolls between affliction and virtue outcomes.
Affliction selection uses DDGC's weighted trait table.
The selected trait is applied to the hero as an active affliction or virtue.
Trait buffs modify hero attributes through the same modifier pipeline as quirks and trinkets.
Focused tests prove affliction is common, virtue is possible but rare, and trait buffs modify hero stats.
Typecheck passes

metadata.notes: Use seeded RNG for deterministic resolution.

title: P5a-1: Parse DDGC curio, trap, and obstacle definitions (contracts)
description:
As a developer, I need the DDGC curio, trap, and obstacle data parsed into the data model so actual game content drives interactions.

Focus: implement the contracts-layer changes.
acceptance_criteria: A CurioRegistry holds all DDGC curio definitions parsed from Curios.csv.
A TrapRegistry holds all DDGC trap definitions parsed from Traps.json.
An ObstacleRegistry holds all DDGC obstacle definitions parsed from Obstacles.json.
At least 3 curios per dungeon region are parsed for QingLong, BaiHu, ZhuQue, and XuanWu.
All 3 trap types and all 5 obstacle types are parsed.
Focused tests prove registry lookup by ID, dungeon-scoped curio filtering, and item interaction overrides are preserved.
Typecheck passes
Changes are scoped to the contracts module.

metadata.notes: Curios.csv uses a multi-row block format per curio and requires custom parsing. Traps.json and Obstacles.json are standard JSON.

title: P5e-3: Wire overstress into battle loop
description: As a developer, I need the battle loop to check for overstress and resolve it so the stress mechanic produces consequences during combat.
acceptance_criteria: When a hero's stress exceeds ATTR_MAX_STRESS, resolve_overstress is called.
The resulting affliction or virtue takes effect immediately with buffs applied and act-outs enabled.
Battle trace records overstress events with the resulting trait.
At least one end-to-end test proves stress damage pushes a hero over threshold, affliction is applied, and act-out occurs on a subsequent turn.
cargo test passes with overstress resolution active.
Typecheck passes

metadata.notes: Integration slice completing the stress to affliction to combat behavior chain.

title: Realign Runtime Effect Resolution To Public Framework APIs (runner)
description:
As a developer, I need encounter execution and runtime helpers to work entirely through the current public `framework_combat` APIs so the game crate no longer depends on private helpers or obsolete return shapes.

Focus: implement the runner-layer changes.
acceptance_criteria: `src/run/encounters.rs` no longer imports private framework functions
Runtime code compiles against the current `resolve_skill` and effect-result APIs
Riposte, deferred-effect, and battle-loop plumbing compile without referencing removed `results` or `deferred` wrapper fields
Typecheck passes
Changes are scoped to the runner module.

title: Realign Runtime Effect Resolution To Public Framework APIs (adapter)
description:
As a developer, I need encounter execution and runtime helpers to work entirely through the current public `framework_combat` APIs so the game crate no longer depends on private helpers or obsolete return shapes.

Focus: implement the adapter-layer changes.
acceptance_criteria: `src/run/encounters.rs` no longer imports private framework functions
Runtime code compiles against the current `resolve_skill` and effect-result APIs
Riposte, deferred-effect, and battle-loop plumbing compile without referencing removed `results` or `deferred` wrapper fields
Typecheck passes
Changes are scoped to the adapter module.

title: P4a-1: Implement HP-threshold conditions (arbiter)
description:
As a developer, I need HpAbove, TargetHpAbove, and TargetHpBelow DDGC conditions implemented so HP-threshold skill gating works correctly instead of being always-on or always-off.

Focus: implement the arbiter-layer changes.
acceptance_criteria: ConditionAdapter evaluates ddgc_hp_above_<threshold>, ddgc_target_hp_above_<threshold>, and ddgc_target_hp_below_<threshold> condition tags.
HP-threshold evaluation reads ATTR_HEALTH and ATTR_MAX_HEALTH from the appropriate actor or target and compares against the fractional threshold.
At least one migrated or fixture skill uses an HP-threshold condition through the adapter.
Focused tests prove condition passes above threshold, fails below threshold, and is deterministic at the exact threshold boundary.
MIGRATION_BLOCKERS.md B-004 status is updated.
Typecheck passes
Changes are scoped to the arbiter module.

metadata.notes: Primary anchor: any boss or hero skill that applies bonus effects when HP is above or below a threshold. ConditionContext already exposes actors HashMap; read HP attributes directly.

title: Reconnect DDGC Conditional Effects Through Supported Condition Plumbing (contracts)
description:
As a developer, I need DDGC conditional skills to express their gating through supported condition structures or game-layer adapters so content compiles without writing removed `EffectNode` fields.

Focus: implement the contracts-layer changes.
acceptance_criteria: Hero or monster content no longer assigns nonexistent `EffectNode` fields
The replacement condition path uses supported framework conditions or the existing DDGC condition adapter seam in game-layer code
Conditional skills that served as documented anchors, including the Hunter condition skills, still participate in tests or runtime scenarios in an observable way
Typecheck passes
Changes are scoped to the contracts module.

title: P4a-2: Implement dungeon-mode condition (contracts)
description:
As a developer, I need the InMode DDGC condition implemented so skills that behave differently in specific dungeon modes can diverge correctly.

Focus: implement the contracts-layer changes.
acceptance_criteria: ConditionAdapter evaluates ddgc_in_mode_<mode> condition tags.
Mode resolution reads from ConditionContext::dungeon and any game-layer mode state.
At least one fixture skill uses an InMode condition.
Focused test proves the condition passes when the current dungeon or mode matches and fails otherwise.
MIGRATION_BLOCKERS.md B-004 status is updated to reflect InMode implementation.
Typecheck passes
Changes are scoped to the contracts module.

metadata.notes: DDGC's InMode is tied to dungeon-specific battle modifiers. The ConditionContext already carries dungeon: Dungeon; extend with an optional mode flag.

title: Repair Scenario And Regression Fixtures Against The Current Runtime Shape (state)
description:
As a developer, I need regression scenarios and focused tests to match the repaired runtime interfaces so they validate current behavior instead of an outdated internal representation.

Focus: implement the state-layer changes.
acceptance_criteria: Scenario files under `src/scenarios/` compile against the repaired runtime interfaces
Focused tests that were asserting on obsolete result wrappers are updated to supported observable outputs
Deterministic trace-oriented coverage remains in place for the repaired battle pipeline where applicable
Typecheck passes
Changes are scoped to the state module.

title: P4a-3: Implement kill-trigger condition (runner)
description:
As a developer, I need an OnKill DDGC condition implemented so skills that trigger bonus effects on enemy death can execute correctly.

Focus: implement the runner-layer changes.
acceptance_criteria: ConditionAdapter evaluates a ddgc_on_kill condition tag.
Kill detection reads from a game-layer kill event tracker that records which actors died this turn.
At least one fixture or migrated skill uses the OnKill condition.
Focused test proves the condition passes on a turn where the actor killed an enemy and fails when no kill occurred.
Typecheck passes
Changes are scoped to the runner module.

metadata.notes: Requires a lightweight kill-event accumulator in the game layer similar to UsageCounters. Add KillTracker to src/run/.

title: P4a-3: Implement kill-trigger condition (arbiter)
description:
As a developer, I need an OnKill DDGC condition implemented so skills that trigger bonus effects on enemy death can execute correctly.

Focus: implement the arbiter-layer changes.
acceptance_criteria: ConditionAdapter evaluates a ddgc_on_kill condition tag.
Kill detection reads from a game-layer kill event tracker that records which actors died this turn.
At least one fixture or migrated skill uses the OnKill condition.

Typecheck passes
Changes are scoped to the arbiter module.

metadata.notes: Requires a lightweight kill-event accumulator in the game layer similar to UsageCounters. Add KillTracker to src/run/.

title: P5a-4: Wire curio, trap, and obstacle into room exploration (cli)
description:
As a developer, I need room exploration events to trigger curio, trap, and obstacle interactions so the dungeon run produces the full exploration cycle.

Focus: implement the cli-layer changes.
acceptance_criteria: RoomKind::Event rooms can carry a curio_id reference.
RoomKind::Corridor rooms can carry optional trap_id and curio_id.
Entering a room with a curio or obstacle produces an interaction outcome in the run trace.
Traversing a corridor with a trap produces a trap outcome in the run trace.
At least one run slice produces curio interaction and trap interaction entries.
cargo test passes with interaction events in the trace.
Typecheck passes
Changes are scoped to the cli module.

metadata.notes: Integration slice. Room generation from Phase 4c should assign curios and traps based on DungeonMapConfig densities.

title: P4a-4: Update condition coverage and close B-004 (docs)

acceptance_criteria: SEMANTIC_GAPS.md SG-002 is updated to reflect all newly implemented conditions.
MIGRATION_BLOCKERS.md B-004 status is changed from Partially resolved to Resolved.
A condition coverage table lists all implemented DDGC conditions versus the original 35+ variants, with remaining ones classified as low-impact deferred or not used in migrated content.
cargo test passes with full condition coverage.
Typecheck passes
Changes are scoped to the docs module.

metadata.notes: Closeout slice. Remaining unimplemented conditions, if any, should be documented as low-priority rather than blockers.

title: P5b-0: Define town building data model (contracts)
description:
As a developer, I need data structures for town buildings, their upgrade trees, and their services so the game layer can represent the between-run meta state.

Focus: implement the contracts-layer changes.
acceptance_criteria: TownBuilding tracks id, building_type, unlock_conditions, and upgrade_trees.
UpgradeTree tracks tree_id and levels.
UpgradeLevel tracks code, cost, and building-specific effects.
TownState tracks current upgrade level per building, gold, and heirloom currencies.
All structs derive Serialize, Deserialize, Clone, and Debug.
Focused tests prove TownState construction and upgrade application are deterministic.
Typecheck passes
Changes are scoped to the contracts module.

metadata.notes: Data-model slice. DDGC buildings have variable upgrade structures such as Abbey sub-trees and StageCoach parallel trees.

title: P4b-0: Wire DamageRange into migrated skill definitions (state)
description:
As a developer, I need migrated skill definitions to carry DamageRange min/max metadata instead of pre-averaged values so the damage policy can choose between fixed and rolled modes.

Focus: implement the state-layer changes.
acceptance_criteria: All hero and monster skill definitions that deal damage store DamageRange::new(min, max) alongside the current EffectNode::damage(average) call.
A lookup or mapping exists from skill ID to its DamageRange.
Existing tests continue passing under FixedAverage policy with no behavior change.
At least 5 migrated skills have verified min/max values matching DDGC source data.
Typecheck passes
Changes are scoped to the state module.

metadata.notes: Do not change EffectNode::damage() calls yet. Add a parallel DamageRange registry keyed by skill ID.

description:

Typecheck passes

metadata.notes: Each .building.json has a unique structure, so the parser must handle building-specific fields generically.

title: P5b-2: Implement town visit cycle (state)
description:
As a developer, I need a town visit phase that occurs between dungeon runs so the meta-game loop is complete.

Focus: implement the state-layer changes.
acceptance_criteria: TownVisit represents a single town phase with available services, hero roster state, and gold and heirloom balances.
perform_town_activity(building_id, activity, hero_id, upgrade_level) resolves a building service.
Town visit produces a trace of activities performed.
At least one end-to-end test proves entering town after a dungeon run, performing a stress heal at the Abbey, reducing hero stress, and deducting gold.
Town visit is deterministic for given state and activity choices.
Typecheck passes
Changes are scoped to the state module.

metadata.notes: Start with Abbey stress heal and StageCoach recruit as the two most critical buildings.

title: P4c-0: Parse DDGC MapGenerator parameters (contracts)
description:
As a developer, I need the DDGC map generation parameters parsed and available at runtime so each dungeon generates maps matching the original structure.

Focus: implement the contracts-layer changes.
acceptance_criteria: A DungeonMapConfig struct exists with fields matching MapGenerator.txt including room counts, corridor counts, gridsize, connectivity, min_final_distance, hallway densities, and room densities.
Four dungeon configs are defined for QingLong, BaiHu, ZhuQue, and XuanWu with parameters extracted from MapGenerator.txt.
At least 2 size variants per dungeon, short and medium, are supported.
Focused tests prove each dungeon config matches the corresponding MapGenerator.txt values.
Typecheck passes
Changes are scoped to the contracts module.

metadata.notes: Data-model slice only. Do not integrate with DefaultRoomGenerator yet.

title: P4c-1: Wire dungeon map configs into room generation (contracts)
description:
As a developer, I need DungeonMapConfig parameters fed into the framework's room generation so generated maps reflect DDGC's dungeon structure.

Focus: implement the contracts-layer changes.
acceptance_criteria: Run or Floor creation reads the appropriate DungeonMapConfig for the current dungeon.
Room generation uses per-dungeon room and corridor counts, connectivity, and grid size.
A deterministic test proves QingLong maps have the correct room count, BaiHu maps have lower connectivity than ZhuQue, and short versus medium maps differ in room count.
cargo test passes with DDGC-configured generation.
Typecheck passes
Changes are scoped to the contracts module.

metadata.notes: May require extending DefaultRoomGenerator parameters or using a custom RoomGenerator implementation. Prefer configuration over code changes.

title: P5b-4: Implement hero XP and leveling (state)
description:
As a developer, I need hero experience and leveling so heroes can progress through resolve levels between dungeon runs.

Focus: implement the state-layer changes.
acceptance_criteria: HeroProgress tracks xp, resolve_level, and hero_level.
XP thresholds match Campaign.json for level_threshold_table and resolve_thresholds.
add_xp(amount) advances hero level and resolve level when thresholds are crossed.
Resolve level gates dungeon access so heroes above resolve level cannot enter low-level dungeons.
Focused tests prove leveling at each threshold boundary.
Typecheck passes
Changes are scoped to the state module.

metadata.notes: DDGC's level system is separate from resolve level. Both use XP from Campaign.json.

title: P5c-0: Define trinket and equipment data model (contracts)
description:
As a developer, I need data structures for trinkets and equipment upgrades so hero stats can be computed from gear rather than hardcoded values.

Focus: implement the contracts-layer changes.
acceptance_criteria: TrinketDefinition tracks id, buffs, hero_class_requirements, rarity, price, limit, and origin_dungeon.
EquipmentDefinition tracks id, hero_class_id, slot, upgrade_level, and stat_modifiers.
AttributeModifier tracks attribute_key and value.
All structs derive Serialize, Deserialize, Clone, and Debug.
Focused tests prove construction and lookup are deterministic.
Typecheck passes
Changes are scoped to the contracts module.

metadata.notes: Trinket buffs reference JsonBuffs.json buff IDs that map to attribute modifiers in the game layer.

title: P4c-2: Parse DDGC encounter pack weights from dungeon .bytes (state)
description:
As a developer, I need the encounter pack weights from DDGC dungeon .bytes files available at runtime so monster composition in rooms matches the original game.

Focus: implement the state-layer changes.
acceptance_criteria: A DungeonEncounterConfig struct captures the weighted encounter definitions from dungeon .bytes files.
Each dungeon's hall, room, and boss encounter packs are registered with correct monster IDs and chances.
At least one dungeon, QingLong, has fully parsed encounter data matching its .bytes source.
Focused tests prove encounter selection produces the expected monster composition for a given seed.
Typecheck passes
Changes are scoped to the state module.

metadata.notes: The dungeon .bytes format uses mash blocks with chance and types fields. Parse into the existing EncounterPackRegistry structure.

title: P5d-0: Define quirk data model and parse DDGC quirks (contracts)
description:
As a developer, I need quirk definitions parsed from JsonQuirks.json so heroes can acquire and be affected by quirks and diseases.

Focus: implement the contracts-layer changes.
acceptance_criteria: QuirkDefinition tracks id, is_positive, is_disease, classification, buffs, incompatible_quirks, and curio_tag.
A QuirkRegistry holds all DDGC quirk definitions.
At least 10 quirks including positive, negative, and disease entries are parsed.
Buffs are resolved through the BuffRegistry from US-913.
Focused tests prove lookup, positive or negative or disease classification, and buff resolution.
Typecheck passes
Changes are scoped to the contracts module.

metadata.notes: Quirks use the same buff ID system as trinkets and depend on the BuffRegistry.

title: P5d-1: Implement quirk application to heroes (state)
description:
As a developer, I need heroes to carry active quirks that modify their attributes so the quirk system actually affects gameplay.

Focus: implement the state-layer changes.
acceptance_criteria: HeroQuirkState tracks active positive quirks, negative quirks, and diseases.
apply_quirk(hero, quirk_id) adds a quirk while enforcing incompatible quirk replacement and category slot limits.
Quirk modifiers are applied alongside equipment and trinket modifiers in compute_hero_stats.
Focused tests prove quirk modifiers change hero attributes, incompatible quirks replace existing ones, and diseases are tracked separately.
Typecheck passes
Changes are scoped to the state module.

metadata.notes: DDGC typically limits heroes to five positive and five negative quirks.

title: P5d-2: Wire quirk acquisition into curio and combat events (state)
description:
As a developer, I need quirks to be acquired from curio interactions and combat events so the hero's quirk profile evolves during runs.

Focus: implement the state-layer changes.
acceptance_criteria: Curio result type Quirk triggers apply_quirk with the result quirk ID.
Curio result type Disease triggers apply_quirk with the disease quirk ID.
Combat events that cause disease trigger apply_quirk.
At least one end-to-end test proves a curio adds a quirk, disease from combat is applied, and quirk modifiers affect subsequent combat stats.
cargo test passes with quirk acquisition active.
Typecheck passes
Changes are scoped to the state module.

metadata.notes: Integration slice connecting the curio system, combat system, and quirk system.

title: P5e-0: Define trait and affliction data model and parse DDGC traits (contracts)
description:
As a developer, I need trait definitions for afflictions and virtues parsed from JsonTraits.json so the overstress system can resolve correctly.

Focus: implement the contracts-layer changes.
acceptance_criteria: TraitDefinition tracks id, overstress_type, buff_ids, combat_start_turn_act_outs, and reaction_act_outs.
ActOutEntry tracks action and weight.
ReactionEntry tracks trigger, probability, and effect.
A TraitRegistry holds all DDGC trait definitions.
At least 2 afflictions and 1 virtue are parsed.
Focused tests prove trait lookup, act-out weight preservation, and reaction probability preservation.
Typecheck passes
Changes are scoped to the contracts module.

metadata.notes: JsonTraits.json uses nested arrays for act-outs and reactions.

title: P5e-2: Implement affliction combat behavior (cli)
description:
As a developer, I need affliction combat behavior implemented so afflicted heroes occasionally disobey orders during combat.

Focus: implement the cli-layer changes.
acceptance_criteria: resolve_act_out(affliction_id, seed) rolls against the weighted act-out table at the start of an afflicted hero's turn.
Possible outcomes include Nothing, IgnoreCommand, ChangePosition, BarkStress, AttackFriendly, and MarkSelf.
Act-out results are recorded in the battle trace.
Focused tests prove Nothing is the most common outcome, other outcomes are possible, and act-out resolution is deterministic for a given seed.
Typecheck passes
Changes are scoped to the cli module.

metadata.notes: DDGC's act-out weights make nothing dominant with smaller chances for disruptive actions.
